title: NO SPOILERS English fandub of 30 opening scene
display_title: NO SPOILERS English fandub of 30 opening scene
wikitext:
[]: [NO SPOILERS] English fandub of 3.0 opening scene!             Quote Post by weeminator24 » Wed Nov 28, 2012 3:11 pm Just go to youtube and serch "Evangelion 3.33 fandub", it should be the first one...

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Nov 28, 2012 3:11 pm god save us from fandubs

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Wed Nov 28, 2012 3:13 pm Xard wrote: god save us from fandubs

[]:             Quote Post by weeminator24 » Wed Nov 28, 2012 3:18 pm It's actually not as bad as you think it would be...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Nov 28, 2012 3:22 pm weeminator24 wrote: It's actually not as bad as you think it would be...

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Wed Nov 28, 2012 3:36 pm It's actually pretty well made.

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Wed Nov 28, 2012 3:39 pm It's not that horrible. Still futile though. One glaring error is Asuka exclaiming "It's an Angel!" when the Mk. 04b activates.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Wed Nov 28, 2012 3:49 pm It's silly, my favorite line is when she screams JESUS!!

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Wed Nov 28, 2012 4:39 pm Surprisingly, it's actually quite good......for a fandub. Still prefer subs any day of the week, primarily cause the actors are taking directions directly from Anno/Tsurumaki etc.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Nov 28, 2012 5:00 pm weeminator24 wrote: It's actually not as bad as you think it would be...

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Nov 28, 2012 8:02 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Sounds about as good as the actual dub...

[]:             Quote Post by alexprevity » Sat Dec 01, 2012 1:02 pm That fandub wasn't bad at all! The sound job was actually pretty impressive. Do people usually build the sound from the ground up with fandubs?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Dec 01, 2012 2:37 pm alexprevity wrote: That fandub wasn't bad at all! The sound job was actually pretty impressive. Do people usually build the sound from the ground up with fandubs?

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Wed Dec 05, 2012 6:54 am This is a really solid, polished effort. I was pleasantly surprised. The actress playing Asuka, in particular, really shines here. And the sound mixing is excellent. A few small critiques, though: - The female actors do a markedly better job than the male actor; his voice feels a little flat for the role and the situation. - At times it can be hard to distinguish between Asuka's voice and Mari's. The actresses sound very similar. I would have opted for a different take on Mari. Maybe I would have chosen a British accent of some sort, given that she's supposed to be British. (Come to think of it, I'm surprised the official English dubs haven't gone in that direction).

[]:             Quote Post by entrodiibob » Wed Dec 05, 2012 10:37 am That was a pretty good dub. Biggest differences between the original and fandub is the original has a better plethora of ambient sounds. Also you just can't pass up using "God's Message" track from the OST.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Thu Dec 06, 2012 4:02 pm Yeah, this wasn't bad at all. Only problem was Kaworu at the end but really nice job. Also Asuka saying "Where's Shinji when you need him?!" was pretty poignant for me. Without any translation in the Japanese version, I thought she was insulting him becuse he was inside that cross thing. But that line of dialogue is telling me she has no beef with him, when she very easily could. But I guess I'd have to know it better in context with the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 06, 2012 6:18 pm shinji_ryoji_89 wrote: Without any translation in the Japanese version, I thought she was insulting him becuse he was inside that cross thing.

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Sat Dec 08, 2012 4:32 pm Reichu wrote: Well, she was insulting him any which way. "DO SOMETHING, BAKA -SHINJI!"

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Dec 08, 2012 4:40 pm Reichu wrote: Well, she was insulting him any which way. "DO SOMETHING, BAKA -SHINJI!"

[]:             Quote Post by Redtophat » Sat Dec 08, 2012 4:50 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Isn't "Baka-Shinji" more a disguised term of endearment than an insult at this point?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Nov 28, 2012 3:11 pm god save us from fandubs
